q: A rectangular garden has a length of 60 m and a width of 40 m. A fence is to be built around the entire garden. If fencing costs £7.50 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the garden is 2 \times (60 + 40) = 2 \times 100 = 200 m. The total cost of the fence is 200 \times £7.50 = £1500.

q: A rectangular parking lot has a length of 150 m and a width of 100 m. A fence is to be built around the entire lot. If fencing costs £9.20 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the lot is 2 \times (150 + 100) = 2 \times 250 = 500 m. The total cost of the fence is 500 \times £9.20 = £4600.

q: A rectangular park has a length of 85 m and a width of 65 m. A fence is to be built around the entire park. If fencing costs £11.00 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the park is 2 \times (85 + 65) = 2 \times 150 = 300 m. The total cost of the fence is 300 \times £11.00 = £3300.

q: A rectangular storage yard has a length of 200 m and a width of 150 m. A fence is to be built around the entire yard. If fencing costs £6.40 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the yard is 2 \times (200 + 150) = 2 \times 350 = 700 m. The total cost of the fence is 700 \times £6.40 = £4480.

q: A rectangular paddock has a length of 110 m and a width of 90 m. A fence is to be built around the entire paddock. If fencing costs £15.50 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the paddock is 2 \times (110 + 90) = 2 \times 200 = 400 m. The total cost of the fence is 400 \times £15.50 = £6200.

q: A rectangular courtyard has a length of 55 m and a width of 45 m. A fence is to be built around the entire courtyard. If fencing costs £14.00 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the courtyard is 2 \times (55 + 45) = 2 \times 100 = 200 m. The total cost of the fence is 200 \times £14.00 = £2800.

q: A rectangular tennis court area has a length of 75 m and a width of 25 m. A fence is to be built around the entire area. If fencing costs £18.50 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the area is 2 \times (75 + 25) = 2 \times 100 = 200 m. The total cost of the fence is 200 \times £18.50 = £3700.

q: A rectangular warehouse site has a length of 125 m and a width of 75 m. A fence is to be built around the entire site. If fencing costs £10.25 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the site is 2 \times (125 + 75) = 2 \times 200 = 400 m. The total cost of the fence is 400 \times £10.25 = £4100.

q: A rectangular swimming pool deck has a length of 40 m and a width of 30 m. A safety fence is to be built around the entire deck. If fencing costs £22.00 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the deck is 2 \times (40 + 30) = 2 \times 70 = 140 m. The total cost of the fence is 140 \times £22.00 = £3080.

q: A rectangular exhibition space has a length of 95 m and a width of 55 m. A temporary fence is to be built around the entire space. If fencing costs £12.00 per metre, what is the total cost of the fence?
e: The perimeter of the space is 2 \times (95 + 55) = 2 \times 150 = 300 m. The total cost of the fence is 300 \times £12.00 = £3600.
